203:
9.11.2021 "Cossack Gay-Fighters."
Semyon Skrepetsky, painting, acrylic on canvas, 90/70 cm.
Two brave Orthodox Cossacks, while on patrol, unexpectedly to themselves committed the sin of sodomy; after the deed they looked around and saw a rainbow. The Cossacks understood that this was not a rainbow but gay-rays, with which Bill Gates irradiates Mother Russia from space. The brave Cossacks did not lose their heads and gouged out each other's eyes, so as never again to fall under the influence of the gay-rays.
204:
11.11.2021
"The Snitch."
Semyon Skrepetsky, acrylic on canvas, 60/50 cm. Painting. An offended Russian patriot writes a denunciation against the Russophobe Semyon Skrepetsky. Based on real events, painted from life.

Writing denunciations against a person you dislike is an ancient Russian, and later Soviet, tradition.
Russians repeatedly wrote denunciations against Semyon Skrepetsky; later, in 2024, they were joined by citizens of Ukraine, who wrote denunciations to the Polish police, asking them to deal with the artist who offends the great president Zelensky.
205:
15.11.2021
"Putin's Metaverse. We Must Defend the Avatars of Russians in the Emerging Metaverse." Semyon Skrepetsky, acrylic on canvas, painting, 60/50 cm.

12 Nov 2021, 21:52
Putin spoke of the authorities' duty to protect the avatars of Russians in metaverses.

The state must take charge of protecting Russians and their virtual doubles in cyberspace, Russian President Vladimir Putin stated at the international conference on artificial intelligence and data analysis, Artificial Intelligence Journey 2021, an RBC correspondent reports.

"The state must take responsibility for storing critically important information. It is no longer a question of ensuring the cybersecurity of the person himself, but also of his virtual double — his avatar within the emerging metaverses," Putin noted.

209:
26.11.2021
"Putin Terrorizes Ukraine." Semyon Skrepetsky, acrylic on canvas, 60/50 cm. Painting.

27 September 2021.
In Russia a criminal case was opened against the commander of a GRU special-forces military unit and his deputy, who raped conscript soldiers in a perverted form.

Journalists managed to obtain internal-use documents of the Russian Ministry of Defense setting out the facts of violent crimes committed by a GRU company commander in Khabarovsk Krai — Major Panov — and his deputy, Captain Yerushevich.

Thus, in November 2020 the major demanded money from a private "for the company's needs," and after a refusal ordered the soldier to strip naked, after which he was beaten, tied to a tree with bound hands, and threatened with having his genitals stapled to the trunk.

Before that, in August 2020, a lance corporal and a private were brought to Panov for an "educational talk," during which the major put a medical glove on a mop handle and began "moving it around the lance corporal's buttocks and groin, threatening to rape him."

Another case recorded in the materials of the Russian Defense Ministry occurred in January 2021, when the company medic, on the orders of Panov and Yerushevich, gave a serviceman an enema, after which he was raped with a mop handle. The captain filmed this on his phone.

The painting is based on a real story when the commander of a fallen soldier gave the parents a sack of onions and said — Thank you for your son, he was a good soldier, he died, here's a sack of onions for him. A sack of onions — the price of a Russian soldier's life.

Semyon Skrepetsky.

The expulsion of women from the male state, or the dream of a Male-Movement member.

MM — the Male Movement, and MS — the Male State. Two radical sects in the territory of the CIS.
The sects unite radical homosexuals, incels and misogynists. The ideology of the sects is the struggle against the female sex.
215:
3.01.2022
"Saint Vladimir, Emperor of Scumbags."
Semyon Skrepetsky,
painting, acrylic on canvas.

Emperor of the Deep Metaverse — Saint Vladimir, god and tsar of his civilization, which he created. In his hands are sacred objects: in his left hand the constitution he wrote, in his right hand the mop of justice, with which he rapes the undesirables. Saint Vladimir possesses unlimited power, and his numerous subjects adore him, since he is the guarantor of the stability of the empire of scumbags.
216:
7.01.2022
"CSTO — Union of Dictators." (Organization of Granddads Stealing the Future)
Semyon Skrepetsky. Painting, acrylic on canvas. 70/60 cm.
In the territory of the former Soviet Union, some time after the collapse of the red empire, a union of dictators formed who solemnly promised each other military and financial aid in case the people in the territories they had seized should revolt. Officially this union was called the "Collective Security Treaty Organization," and by the legend the treaty's members must help each other in case of an external threat. But the real point of the organization is to help one another suppress popular unrest if the local army refuses to shoot its own citizens.
This painting depicts the core membership of the union of bloody dictators — Lukashenko, Putin, Nazarbayev, and Pashinyan reaching out his little hands toward them.

Skrepetsky Picture Gallery.
217:
8.01.2022
The Emperor's Cook. Pantheon of Scumbags #2
Putin's cook — Yevgeny Prigozhin. In Soviet times this figure spent 11 years in places of confinement for committing especially grave crimes. After his release, thanks to his cunning and special cruelty, he became a crime boss and the right hand of the emperor of scumbags. In the Empire of Scumbags he holds the post of minister of crime, heading the ministry of killers, thieves and terrorists. All illegal machinations the emperor commits pass through Prigozhin's hands. He is also the general director of the troll factory; at his command hacker attacks are carried out on government websites around the world.
Painted on canvas, acrylic. 60/50 cm. Artist Semyon Skrepetsky.
218:
10.01.2022
Warlord Shoigu. Pantheon of Scumbags #3
Painting.
Semyon Skrepetsky. Acrylic on canvas. 50/35 cm.
Sergei Shoigu — minister of attack, general of the invincible Russian army. An honored hero of victory parades, he never served in the army but has a military profession — driver of a reindeer sleigh.
Shoigu's main achievements can be considered the heroic storming of the cardboard Reichstag in 2017 and the construction of the Temple of War.
He has a close friendship with the emperor of scumbags, often retreating with him for long stretches into the Siberian taiga. No one knows what the emperor and the warlord do, hidden from human eyes. There are versions that they are homosexual lovers, but some believe the emperor and the warlord hide from people to perform a magic ritual and drink the blood of a black dog.
219:
13.10.2022
Igor Sechin. Pantheon of Scumbags #4
Semyon Skrepetsky. Painting on canvas, acrylic, 50/35 cm.
Igor Sechin — the emperor's effective manager. Minister of oil, he manages the extraction and sale of oil in the empire of scumbags. An old friend and brother-in-arms of the emperor. Igor Sechin holds the post of general director of the Rosneft company and, with his brilliant management, managed to drive the oil-extraction company into multibillion debts that many times exceed the value of the Rosneft company itself.
The economy of the empire of scumbags rests on the extraction and sale of oil and gas.

Semyon Skrepetsky, acrylic on canvas, painting. 60/50 cm. Russia issued an ultimatum to the entire civilized world, demanding that countries not resist in case Russia should express a desire to attack and plunder them.

20.01.2022 Lavrov the Knight #5
Semyon Skrepetsky. The painting is done on canvas in acrylic. Dimensions 50/35 cm.
Sergei Lavrov, aka Lavrov the Knight, aka the Knight's Move. Diplomat, negotiator, alcoholic and boor. When Emperor Vladimir wants to insult someone and break off diplomatic relations, he makes a "Knight's Move," that is, sends Sergei Lavrov to the negotiations, and he handles his mission splendidly. Lavrov calls names, swears, intimidates; there was once a case when, during international negotiations, Sergei Lavrov climbed onto the table, took off his trousers and laid a huge stinking heap on the negotiating table.

Semyon Skrepetsky, painting, acrylic on canvas, 50/40 cm.

Looting by the Russian army during the 2022 invasion of Ukraine — mass cases of robbery and theft, recorded by international observers and local residents, committed by Russian troops in the occupied territories of Ukraine. International organizations such as Human Rights Watch, as well as Ukrainian, European and Russian independent media and NGOs, document and investigate cases of looting and the transport into Russia and Belarus of looted personal belongings and industrial equipment.
